title: Ledger display formatter formatMoney (ADR 0004)
content: ai-bookkeeper ledger now has src/features/ledger/lib/format.ts: formatMoney(value, currency, locale?) — the display pair to the roundMoney/arithmetic stack. Design: (1) Intl.NumberFormat with style:'currency' is the single authority for symbol placement, grouping, and minor-unit precision — no hard-coded symbol or decimal count, so JPY renders 0dp, USD 2dp, de-DE puts € after the number with comma decimal. (2) locale defaults to 'en-US'. (3) value is normalized through roundMoney(value,currency) BEFORE formatting, so display can never disagree with stored value by float drift and -0 normalizes to 0 (no '-$0.00'). (4) reuses ADR 0003's MoneyArithmeticError for fail-loud rejection of non-finite values and mixed-currency tagged Money objects (.code 'not-finite'|'mixed-currency'); blank/unknown currency throws RangeError via roundMoney/minorUnits/Intl. Accepts bare number or tagged Money (MoneyInput). Exported via features/ledger barrel. Reviewer's canonical check: formatMoney(addMoney(0.1,0.2,'USD'),'USD')==='$0.30' exactly. Rationale: let Intl derive fraction digits rather than forcing minorUnits() — ICU and our MINOR_UNITS table agree for standard ISO codes, and roundMoney already pinned the value, so no second source of truth. Residual risk: Intl output is ICU-version-sensitive (UNVERIFIED across all deploy targets). Tests: 42/42 total (8 new format tests).

title: Release gate policy: a pre-existing repo-wide lint error blocks merge even for clean feature work
content: On ai-bookkeeper, the release rule is build + lint + tests ALL green on the POST-MERGE state before merge. A pre-existing, unrelated lint error elsewhere in the repo (observed: src/app/page.tsx @next/next/no-html-link-for-pages, an  that should be next/link Link, from commit d5f670c) will fail `eslint .` and therefore block merge of an otherwise-clean feature — even when the feature's own changed files lint clean. Correct handling per instructions: do NOT merge, escalate via manual_review with the specific fix scoped as a task. Practical implication: when scoping ledger work, budget a small follow-on task for repo-level lint hygiene if the gate must be green, and surface the pre-existing error to the user as a decision (fix-then-merge vs. waive-and-ticket).

ADR 0004 formatMoney handoff: verify+commit held; committed 12bc223, merge human-gated on pre-existing page.tsx lint
content: Team-mode handoff on ai-bookkeeper: Architect shipped ADR 0004 formatMoney to the working tree but left it UNCOMMITTED (format.ts + format.test.ts untracked, ledger/index.ts modified, ADR 0004 added). Dev Clone value-add was verify+commit (NOT re-implement), matching the ADR 0002/0003 pattern. Verified: npm test 42/42 (8 new format tests), tsc --noEmit clean, eslint on the 3 changed files clean. Scope checks PASSED: arithmetic.ts/round.ts already tracked so the barrel re-export keeps a fresh checkout compiling (no cross-work-order gap); the README.md modification described ADR-0003 arithmetic (not format) so it was EXCLUDED from the scoped commit. Committed only format.ts/format.test.ts/index.ts/docs/adr/0004 as 12bc223 on main, unpushed (review_each_pr → human merge gate). Merge blocked because release rule requires `eslint .` all-green and a PRE-EXISTING unrelated error persists in src/app/page.tsx (@next/next/no-html-link-for-pages, , from commit d5f670c) — escalated via manual_review rather than auto-fixed (out-of-scope constraint). The page.tsx lint error has now blocked two consecutive ledger work orders; it should be ticketed/fixed independently to stop gating clean feature work.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 19:04 UTC
ADR 0004 formatMoney reviewed APPROVE (12bc223) — display normalizes via roundMoney, no store/display drift; page.tsx lint still gating
content: Reviewed commit 12bc223 (ADR 0004: formatMoney in src/features/ledger/lib/format.ts) on ai-bookkeeper. Verdict APPROVE, no security/correctness/critical blockers. Key correctness property: formatMoney normalizes the value through roundMoney (half-even, ADR 0002) BEFORE Intl.NumberFormat, so the rendered string cannot drift from the stored canonical value; because input is pre-canonicalized to minor units, Intl's own rounding mode is irrelevant. Notably this does NOT inherit the half-up-at-scale-vs-half-even gap flagged in the ADR 0003 arithmetic review, because formatMoney routes the raw value straight through roundMoney with no Math.round pre-scaling. Fail-loud: non-finite/mixed-currency throw MoneyArithmeticError, blank/invalid currency throws RangeError via roundMoney/Intl. Gate re-run green: npm test 42/42 (8 new format tests), tsc clean, eslint on the 3 changed files clean. Build-integrity gate PASSED: all barrel-re-exported siblings (amount/round/arithmetic/ingest/format) are git-tracked, so a clean checkout compiles (no untracked-sibling trap like ADR 0002's b29eeaa). Two LOW non-blocking notes: (1) format.ts:88-92 roundMoney(MINOR_UNITS table, 2dp fallback) vs Intl(CLDR) precision can diverge for non-standard currency codes — documented/accepted for ISO-4217 set, single-source precision if coverage expands; (2) format.ts:26-54 resolveValue duplicates arithmetic.ts:48-80 resolveOperand near-verbatim, extract shared helper. ADJACENT/out-of-scope: src/app/page.tsx:9:11 @next/next/no-html-link-for-pages is pre-existing (commit d5f670c) and now blocks the all-green release gate across TWO consecutive ledger work orders — must be ticketed/fixed independently; reviewer does NOT flag it as a finding against the feature.

ai-bookkeeper @ 12bc223: postcss CVE STILL OPEN (3rd scan, no overrides), now 2 audit entries; new unused @google/generative-ai dep
content: Re-scan at HEAD 12bc223 (after de4137b release + 12bc223 formatMoney). postcss XSS GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (<8.5.10) STILL OPEN — overrides block STILL not added to package.json. Top-level postcss is 8.5.15 (devDep ^8.5.15) but next@16.2.6 nests postcss@8.4.31 at package-lock.json:3486, unreachable by a top-level bump. NEW: npm audit now reports 2 moderate (postcss AND next) — but next.via=['postcss'], same single root cause surfaced twice, NOT a new CVE. Fix remains the one-line overrides pin {"postcss":">=8.5.10"}; npm's suggested next@9.3.3 is a wrong breaking downgrade. Ranked High per known-CVE policy, real reachability low (build-time CSS stringify). NEW LOW finding: @google/generative-ai ^0.24.1 added to package.json dependencies (line 13) but NOT imported anywhere in src — all 'Gemini' hits are marketing copy/future-work comments. Unused prod dep = needless supply-chain surface; remove until server integration lands, and when it does ensure GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS stay server-side (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix). Rest of source CLEAN: 0 secrets (only .env.example tracked; .env/.gcp/*.pem gitignored), 0 risky patterns (no eval/exec/innerHTML/dangerouslySetInnerHTML/process.env), no app/api routes. New format.ts (ADR 0004 formatMoney) is pure/fail-loud, locale+currency only flow into Intl.NumberFormat — no security surface. COVERAGE GAP this run: Obsidian shared-vault tools were unavailable, could not consult/write team knowledge graph.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 19:11 UTC
ai-bookkeeper v0.5.0 release HELD @ 12bc223 — red lint trunk blocker (pre-existing from v0.4.0)
content: Release run at HEAD 12bc223 (formatMoney, ADR 0004 — one feat commit since v0.4.0/de4137b). Already on main, security-cleared. PREPARED bump: 0.4.0 -> 0.5.0 MINOR (git log v0.4.0..HEAD = 1 feat, 0 breaking; pre-1.0 policy => minor). HELD — did NOT tag/bump/changelog because the full quality gate is RED on lint. Gate results: tsc --noEmit clean; tsx --test 42/42 pass; next build success; eslint . => 1 ERROR at src/app/page.tsx:9:11 (@next/next/no-html-link-for-pages — raw  must be next/link). PROVENANCE: error was introduced in 885afae (visual v2 'Ruled Ledger') which shipped INSIDE v0.4.0 — it escaped the prior release gate; the new formatMoney increment does NOT touch page.tsx. As last quality gate, do not tag over red trunk even when failure pre-exists the increment. FIX (handed off via manual_review): add `import Link from next/link`, line 9  -> , line 26  -> ; only the /-route anchor trips the rule (#hash and /docs/*.md anchors are exempt). LESSON: next build does NOT run eslint by default in Next 16 — a green build can still hide a red lint, so lint must be run as a SEPARATE gate step before tagging (this is exactly how v0.4.0 shipped with a lint error). Also uncommitted README.md doc tweak to fold into the release commit. Standing debt carried: postcss GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 nested via next@16.2.6, fix = package.json overrides pin (non-blocking). Obsidian shared-vault tools unavailable again this run.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-28 19:16 UTC
ai-bookkeeper: formatMoney documented under CHANGELOG [Unreleased] while v0.5.0 release held
content: Synced docs for the formatMoney feature (commit 12bc223, ADR 0004 — the display-side pair to roundMoney/addMoney). KEY DECISION: because the Release Manager HELD the v0.5.0 release (red lint trunk, src/app/page.tsx:9 no-html-link-for-pages, pre-existing from 885afae/v0.4.0), I documented formatMoney under the existing CHANGELOG '## [Unreleased]' section rather than fabricating a [0.5.0] heading/tag. Committed-but-unversioned trunk work belongs in [Unreleased]; the Release Manager pipeline promotes it to a versioned heading when the tag is cut. Also updated README ledger/lib structure line to add 'display formatting'. Both docs moved together, code untouched. Watermark de4137b -> 12bc223. LESSON: when a release is held but the feature is already committed to trunk, the Docs Steward still documents it (it IS shipped to trunk) — but parks it in [Unreleased], decoupling docs-currency from the release tag. Watch for duplication when v0.5.0 finally promotes the entry.
